changeSet.author: migration-team
changeSet.comment:
Add unique constraint on (funding_value_id, contributor_type, name) in pafs_core_funding_contributors. Enables the application to use a single Prisma upsert instead of findFirst + create/update.
Contributors with the same type and name across multiple financial years each have a distinct funding_value_id (one funding_value row per project per year), so the constraint correctly scopes uniqueness within a single year's funding value.
NULL handling: PostgreSQL treats each NULL as distinct from every other NULL in a unique constraint. Rows where funding_value_id IS NULL (orphaned legacy rows) will never violate this constraint regardless of how many share the same contributor_type and name — each NULL is considered a unique value.
Cross-proposal rows: contributors with the same contributor_type and name that belong to different proposals have different funding_value_id values and therefore different constraint keys — no conflict.
The only true violation would be two rows with identical non-null (funding_value_id, contributor_type, name) — i.e. the same contributor written twice into the same financial year's funding value. That is the race condition this constraint is designed to prevent.
The single-column index on funding_value_id is dropped because the new compound unique index covers it as a left-prefix. This changeset runs before data migration so the table is empty.
